_doc: FROZEN Elo anchor definition for the training loop. The 1524-vs-846 drift was caused by DIFFERENT Stockfish binaries; pinning the binary + using fixed NODES (not wall-time) makes every anchor bit-identical on any machine. Do not change these values once games have been rated against them — add new higher anchors instead.
sf_build: compiled from source, tag sf_16.1, ARCH=x86-64-sse41-popcnt (same binary local + CARC)
scale_pin: A_low == 1320 (defined); all other Elos are relative to it via BayesElo

A_high.role: high bracket (brackets the ~1638 pretrained net from above)
A_high._calibrate: Pick the node count whose strength is ~1700 (so pretrained ~1638 scores ~45%). Run a >=100-game A_low<->A_high round-robin, set elo via BayesElo with A_low pinned=1320, then FREEZE both nodes and elo. (Preliminary: nodes=1 ~1688, but noisy; calibrate 1/64/256/1024 and pick the cleanest ~1700.)

A_2000.role: FUTURE headroom (add only when the model exceeds ~1700)
A_2000._calibrate: node count calibrated to ~2000 vs the frozen A_low/A_high; never moves the existing anchors.

model_eval_protocol.rule: measure each model against the bracketing anchor(s) where it scores 30-70% (tightest CI)

preliminary_12game_ratings_sf1320._note: From the 12-game probes (wide +-90 CI, overlapping). NOT final; superseded once the 100-game round-robin runs.
